title: Oscars 2026: Horror Wins Big as Sinners, Frankenstein, and Weapons Take Home Gold
text: Horror genre films dominated the 98th Academy Awards, with three standout winners taking home multiple awards at the March 15 ceremony in Los Angeles. Sinners Leads the Pack: Ryan Coogler's vampire period piece "Sinners" earned four Oscars from a record 16 nominations: Best Actor: Michael B. Jordan, who played identical twin brothers Smoke and Stack; Best Original Screenplay: Ryan Coogler; Best Original Score: Ludwig Göransson; Best Cinematography: Autumn Durald Arkapaw. Jordan expressed his gratitude during his speech, saying he was thankful for those who "bet on me" throughout his career and pledged to "keep stepping up." Other Horror Victories: Amy Madigan won Best Supporting Actress for her role as Aunt Gladys in "Weapons", directed by Zac Cregger. She credited the filmmaker for writing "a dream part" for her character. Guillermo del Toro's "Frankenstein" secured three technical awards: Best Costume Design, Best Makeup and Hairstyling, and Best Production Design—categories where del Toro's signature visual aesthetic consistently shines. The strong showing marks the genre's best recognition since "The Silence of the Lambs" won in 1991.
